strategy: Hit with Ice - he takes +30% from it and resists 60% of Fire.

He interrupt-casts a -75% Tower Shield on Round 1 and replaces it every time he is damaged, so either strip it with a ward-removal (which does not make him replace it) or hit hard enough to overpower the -75%.
Skip traps - a trap on him just makes him Cleanse Ward it off.

He hits the whole team for 150 Star every second round (every round once his minions die); the damage order alternates sides each time, and a warded wizard blocks the spread but gets their wards stripped.
At only 1,430 health, set a blade or two and end it with one strong Ice hit; clearing his minions stops the reshielding and makes him drop his own wards.
cheatNotes: He opens with a -75% Tower Shield on himself on Round 1.
Any hit that damages him makes him instantly replace that Tower Shield, so chip damage is wasted.
Ward-removal spells (Steal Ward, Cleanse Ward) take the Tower Shield off WITHOUT making him replace it - use one before your hit.
The reshielding stops once all his minions are defeated.
Trap him and he Cleanse Wards it off himself, so traps do not stick.
Every second round he hits the whole team for 150 Star damage.
The Star hit alternates which side it starts from each time it fires.
Once his minions are dead he casts that 150 Star hit every round instead of every other.
A wizard with any non-absorb ward stops the Star spread from reaching others, but Kaizoku then strips all wards from that wizard and himself (unless they were the last to be hit).
Once his minions fall he removes his own wards, leaving himself exposed for a finishing hit.
